title: Problem with 3-way joins and the USING clause
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, age INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER, content TEXT, FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
CREATE TABLE comments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, post_id INTEGER, user_id INTEGER, comment TEXT, FOREIGN KEY(post_id) REFERENCES posts(id), FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO users (name, age) VALUES ('Alice', 30), ('Bob', 25), ('Charlie', NULL);
INSERT INTO posts (user_id, content) VALUES (1, 'First Post'), (2, 'Second Post');
SELECT u.name, p.content, c.comment FROM users u JOIN posts p ON u.id = p.user_id JOIN comments c ON p.id = c.post_id WHERE u.age IS NOT NULL;
